doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_sub1sp1 (mpfr/src/sub1sp.c L138-L320): same-precision same-sign subtraction fast path for p < GMP_NUMB_BITS (= p in [1,63] on x86_64), i.e. single-limb mantissas with non-zero sub-limb padding (sh = 64 - p > 0). The dispatcher mpfr_sub1sp routes here when both operands have the same precision and that precision is < 64. Mirrors the C structure: convert mantissas to 64-bit C-limb form (mant << sh), handle Case A (equal exponents → exact subtraction with possible sign flip / cancellation / clz normalisation), Case B (different exponents, swap to bx > cx, then B1 d<64 with two's-complement negation of neglected bits / B2 d>=64 splitting on bp0 > HIGHBIT vs bp0 == HIGHBIT). Underflow check before rounding (RNDN narrowed to RNDZ when |a| <= 2^(emin-2)). Five-mode rounding with sign-aware truncate/add-one-ulp; ternary sign reflects rounded-exact direction. The single-limb arithmetic uses BigInt mod-2^64 masking; cancellation handled via clz; signed-zero distinction preserved for RNDD-zero.
c_signature: static int mpfr_sub1sp1(mpfr_ptr a, mpfr_srcptr b, mpfr_srcptr c, mpfr_rnd_t rnd_mode, mpfr_prec_t p)
divergence_from_c: Static C linkage dropped (exercised via mpfr_sub at prec < 64). C asserts p < GMP_NUMB_BITS via MPFR_ASSERTD; TS throws MPFRError('EPREC') outside [1, 63]. C's mp_limb_t bp[0]/cp[0] are 64-bit unsigned ints; TS uses BigInt with mod-2^64 masking. C count_leading_zeros macro replaced by a TS clz64 helper. Result return shape replaces in-place mutation of a. Signed-zero cancellation (RNDD → -0, else +0) explicit per src/core.ts.
refs: mpfr/src/sub1sp.c L138-L320 — verbatim C reference body.
src/ops/sub1sp1n.ts — sister op (p == 64); same structure, sh = 0.
src/ops/add1sp1.ts — companion op (add direction, same prec range).
src/core.ts — locked schema (MPFR, RoundingMode, Result, Ternary, Sign).
CLAUDE.md 'Signed zero is real' — RNDD cancellation gives -0.
CLAUDE.md 'Ternary flag is the sign of (rounded - exact)' — direction discipline.
